name: Medeina
description: Medeina is a Lithuanian goddess of forests, trees, and animals. She is often depicted as a young, beautiful woman, sometimes dressed as a huntress, accompanied by wolves or other wild animals. She is considered a protector of the wilderness and a guardian of the creatures that dwell within it. Medeina is seen as a solitary figure, preferring the company of nature to that of humans. While she is generally benevolent, she can be fierce in her protection of the forest and its inhabitants. She is invoked for successful hunts, safe passage through the woods, and the health and well-being of animals. Some accounts portray her as a huntress herself, while others emphasize her role as a nurturing mother figure to the forest and its creatures. Her power lies in her deep connection to the natural world, granting her influence over the growth of plants, the behavior of animals, and the overall balance of the ecosystem. Offerings to Medeina often include items found in the forest, such as flowers, berries, and feathers. The rustling of leaves in the wind is sometimes interpreted as her voice, and the sight of a deer or wolf is seen as a sign of her presence. Medeina represents the untamed beauty and power of the natural world, reminding people of their connection to the wilderness and the importance of respecting its delicate balance.
subtitle: Goddess of Forests, Animals, and the Hunt

keyMyths.title: Medeina's Guardianship
keyMyths.description: Medeina is said to protect the forests and all the creatures within, fiercely defending them from those who would harm them. Her wrath is swift and unforgiving to those who disrespect the balance of nature.

keyMyths.title: The Hunter's Blessing
keyMyths.description: Hunters would offer prayers and gifts to Medeina before a hunt, seeking her blessing for a successful and respectful endeavor. A bountiful hunt was seen as a sign of her favor.

keyMyths.title: The Lost Child
keyMyths.description: A tale tells of a lost child guided back to safety by Medeina, who appeared in the form of a white wolf. This story highlights her benevolent nature and protectiveness.

sources.author: Gintaras Beresnevičius
